records.ety: [OE. herbe, erbe, OF. herbe, erbe, F. herbe, L. herba; perh. akin to Gr. forbh` food, pasture, fe`rbein to feed.]

defs: A plant whose stem does not become woody and permanent, but dies, at least down to the ground, after flowering.
notes: ☞ Annual herbs live but one season; biennial herbs flower the second season, and then die; perennial herbs produce new stems year after year.

quotes: And flocks Grazing the tender herb.
